notes.note: Greek city in Lucania (southern Italy); a federate ally of Rome, with a treaty under which it could grant its citizenship to outsiders. Archias's enrolment there was the basis for his Roman citizenship under the lex Plautia Papiria.

Law of 89 BC granting Roman citizenship to anyone who had been enrolled in an allied federated city and had a domicile in Italy at the time of the law, provided he made a declaration to the praetor within 60 days. Archias's claim to citizenship rested on this law.

From section 12 onwards Cicero abandons the bare legal case and delivers what is remembered as the great defence of literary culture --- the famous "haec studia adulescentiam alunt" passage at section 16.
Cicero's catalogue of cities claiming Homer (Smyrna, Chios, Colophon, Salamis, Rhodes, Argos, Athens) is the locus classicus of the "seven cities of Homer" tradition.
